title: Decadent Brazilian Beef Stew
description: A rich and indulgent Brazilian dish featuring lean minced beef, this stew is perfect for a special occasion, it is also keto friendly.
instructions: Heat the claypot over medium heat and add the minced beef to brown, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the beef broth, red wine, tomato paste, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Use a spoon to deglaze the bottom of the pot, scraping up any browned bits.
Simmer the stew, uncovered, for an additional 30 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
